concepts_list.name: domain_Omega
concepts_list.description: The main computational domain in the physical space, defined as a subset of R^d.
concepts_list.justification: The domain of interest is defined as a region Ω ⊂ R^d. This is explicitly stated in the text as the domain of interest and is referred to throughout the weak formulation.

concepts_list.name: boundary_Gamma
concepts_list.description: The boundary of the domain Ω, defined as the closure of Ω minus Ω itself, denoted by Γ.
concepts_list.justification: The domain boundary ∂Ω ≡ Γ is defined as the closure of Ω minus Ω, and is composed of disjoint regions Γ_u and Γ_t. This is explicitly stated in the paper.

concepts_list.name: displacement_field_u
concepts_list.description: The primal unknown field (temperature or displacement) that is sought in the weak formulation.
concepts_list.justification: The variable u is used to denote the primal variable in both thermal and elastostatic problems, and is found in the weak form as the solution to be determined. It is used in both the left-hand and right-hand sides of the equations.

concepts_list.name: test_function_v
concepts_list.description: The test function used in the Galerkin formulation, belonging to the function space V.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol v appears in the weak form as a test function, with the condition ∀v ∈ V, and is used in integrals over Ω and Γ_t. It is a standard element in variational formulations.

concepts_list.name: thermal_conductivity_kappa
concepts_list.description: The thermal conductivity tensor, which appears in the diffusion term of the weak form.
concepts_list.justification: The thermal conductivity κ is explicitly mentioned as a given parameter in the thermal boundary value problem and appears in the gradient term ∇(κ∇u).

concepts_list.name: heat_source_f
concepts_list.description: The internal heat source term in the thermal problem, appearing in the right-hand side of the weak form.
concepts_list.justification: The heat source f is explicitly mentioned and appears in the integral over Ω as v f dΩ in the weak form of the thermal problem.

concepts_list.name: heat_flux_q_bar
concepts_list.description: The prescribed heat flux on the Neumann boundary Γ_t, appearing in the boundary integral.
concepts_list.justification: The prescribed heat flux q̄ is mentioned and appears in the boundary integral over Γ_t as v q̄ dΓ in the thermal weak form.

concepts_list.name: function_space_V
concepts_list.description: The test function space, which contains functions v used in the Galerkin method, with Dirichlet boundary conditions on Γ_u.
concepts_list.justification: The function space V is explicitly defined as the space of test functions, and v ∈ V is required in the weak form. It is used in the variational formulation and is associated with the boundary conditions.

concepts_list.name: neumann_boundary_Gamma_t
concepts_list.description: The part of the boundary where Neumann boundary conditions (prescribed flux) are applied, denoted as Γ_t.
concepts_list.justification: The boundary Γ_t is explicitly defined as the region where Neumann boundary conditions (prescribed heat flux) are prescribed, and appears in the boundary integral of the weak form.

domain_Omega.description:
The main computational domain in the physical space, defined as a subset of R^d.
JUSTIFICATION:
The domain of interest is defined as a region Ω ⊂ R^d. This is explicitly stated in the text as the domain of interest and is referred to throughout the weak formulation.

The domain boundary ∂Ω ≡ Γ is defined as the closure of Ω minus Ω, and is composed of disjoint regions Γ_u and Γ_t. This is explicitly stated in the paper.

The test function space, which contains functions v used in the Galerkin method, with Dirichlet boundary conditions on Γ_u.

The function space V is explicitly defined as the space of test functions, and v ∈ V is required in the weak form. It is used in the variational formulation and is associated with the boundary conditions.

The part of the boundary where Neumann boundary conditions (prescribed flux) are applied, denoted as Γ_t.

The boundary Γ_t is explicitly defined as the region where Neumann boundary conditions (prescribed heat flux) are prescribed, and appears in the boundary integral of the weak form.
